name: MCP Asana Server
description: Asana's official hosted MCP server, now V2 at https://mcp.asana.com/v2/mcp (GA 2026-02-04, Streamable HTTP, OAuth with pre-registered apps, workspace-scoped authorization); the v1/beta SSE server was shut down 2026-05-11. Exposes task, project, and status tools. Carries a notable security history: in June 2025 a flawed tenant-isolation check in the beta server exposed data of ~1,000 organizations to other tenants (Jun 5-17), prompting a two-week outage and a rearchitected V2.

evidence.source: Asana Developers - Using Asana's MCP Server
evidence.value: Reliable task creation, assignment, and querying built on the Asana REST API; documented flows include finding incomplete tasks by due date and retrieving project status

evidence.source: Asana Forum Changelog - V2 MCP server GA
evidence.value: V2 GA introduced an optimized tool set, Streamable HTTP support, a new client registration model, and workspace-scoped authorizations

tool_set_quality.methodology: Tool design and coverage review of the V2 release

evidence.source: Asana API - Search Tasks
evidence.value: Task search builds on the workspace search API with typeahead and filter support

evidence.source: Asana API Rate Limits
evidence.value: Subject to Asana API rate limits (per-minute quotas varying by plan)

evidence.source: Asana Developers - Integrating with Asana's MCP Server
evidence.value: Standard API errors propagate to the client; tools/list discovery lets agents adapt to the available tool set

evidence.source: BleepingComputer - Asana warns MCP AI feature exposed customer data to other orgs
evidence.value: A logic flaw in the MCP server's tenant-isolation check (discovered 2025-06-04, exposure window Jun 5-17, 2025) let users access other organizations' tasks, project metadata, team details, comments, and uploaded files; ~1,000 customers including Fortune 500 companies were potentially affected. Not an external hack - an implementation flaw

evidence.source: Asana Forum Changelog - V2 MCP server GA
evidence.value: The rearchitected V2 (GA 2026-02-04) added workspace-scoped authorizations and a new client registration model; the flawed beta architecture was fully retired when v1 shut down on 2026-05-11

tenant_isolation.methodology: Review of the June 2025 cross-tenant exposure incident, its root cause, and the V2 rearchitecture; no cross-tenant incidents reported on V2 as of 2026-07-09

evidence.source: Nudge Security - Asana MCP server data exposure incident
evidence.value: Asana took the MCP server offline immediately upon discovery (2025-06-04 discovery, service restored 2025-06-17) and notified affected organizations - but the flaw had been live since the feature launched 2025-05-01 and was found by Asana, not caught pre-release

incident_history_and_response.methodology: Assessment of detection, containment, and remediation of the June 2025 incident

evidence.source: Asana Developers - Integrating with Asana's MCP Server
evidence.value: V2 uses OAuth with apps pre-registered in the Asana developer console (client ID/secret); Dynamic Client Registration is deliberately not supported, tightening who can connect

evidence.source: Asana Forum Changelog - V2 MCP server GA
evidence.value: V2 authorizations are workspace-scoped; admin app-management controls to govern MCP access are available only on Enterprise+ and Legacy Enterprise tiers - other tiers must contact support to block the MCP app

workspace_access_control.methodology: Access control and admin governance review

evidence.source: Asana Developers - Using Asana's MCP Server
evidence.value: Agents can create and assign tasks and modify work items within the authorizing user's permissions

evidence.source: Asana Audit Log API
evidence.value: Audit log API exists but is Enterprise-tier; during the 2025 incident, affected customers were advised to review logs for MCP access themselves - MCP-specific audit visibility remains limited on lower tiers

evidence.source: BleepingComputer - Asana warns MCP AI feature exposed customer data to other orgs
evidence.value: The June 2025 incident was itself a privacy breach: task data, project metadata, team details, comments/discussions, and uploaded files from ~1,000 organizations were exposed to other tenants' MCP sessions and AI-generated summaries

cross_tenant_exposure_history.methodology: Assessment of realized (not just theoretical) cross-tenant privacy impact; V2 rearchitecture credited but history weighted

evidence.source: MCP Data Flow
evidence.value: Task titles, descriptions, comments, and project plans are sent to the LLM provider as part of normal operation

evidence.source: Asana API - Users
evidence.value: Assignee names, emails, and workload/activity signals are accessible within the authorized workspace

evidence.source: LLM Provider Policies
evidence.value: Work management data retrieved via MCP is processed by the connected LLM provider under that provider's privacy policy

evidence.source: Asana Developers - Using Asana's MCP Server
evidence.value: Only Enterprise+ / Legacy Enterprise admins can manage MCP access via app management; super admins on other tiers must go through support to block the app

admin_privacy_controls.methodology: Admin control availability review across tiers

evidence.source: Asana Developers - Using Asana's MCP Server
evidence.value: First-party docs cover the V2 endpoint, OAuth setup, supported clients (Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, VS Code, Claude Code, Codex), and v1-to-v2 migration

evidence.source: BleepingComputer - Asana warns MCP AI feature exposed customer data to other orgs
evidence.value: Asana notified affected organizations privately with communication forms but issued no public statement; the incident became public through press reporting and third-party security analyses

evidence.source: Nudge Security - Asana MCP server data exposure incident
evidence.value: Third-party researchers, not Asana, provided the most detailed public guidance for affected customers (log review, checking AI summaries for cross-org data)

incident_disclosure.methodology: Assessment of incident disclosure practices versus public-disclosure norms

evidence.source: Asana Audit Log API
evidence.value: Task changes appear in Asana activity; deeper audit visibility requires Enterprise audit log access

evidence.source: Asana Developers - MCP Server
evidence.value: Closed-source hosted service; the tenant-isolation flaw class that caused the 2025 incident is not externally auditable

evidence.source: Asana Forum Changelog - V2 MCP server GA
evidence.value: V2 GA announced 2026-02-04 with a clear migration path and an explicit v1/beta shutdown date (2026-05-11), communicated via changelog and developer docs

evidence.source: Asana Developers - Using Asana's MCP Server
evidence.value: Hosted endpoint with browser OAuth for supported clients; custom clients must pre-register an app in the developer console since DCR is not supported

evidence.source: Nudge Security - Asana MCP server data exposure incident
evidence.value: Stable on Asana's production API, but the service has taken one full ~2-week outage (Jun 5-17, 2025) when pulled offline for the incident

evidence.source: Asana Developer Platform
evidence.value: Performance tracks the Asana REST API; V2's Streamable HTTP transport is the current remote-server standard

evidence.source: Asana Developers - Using Asana's MCP Server
evidence.value: Covers tasks, projects, sections, and status reporting; an optimized (deliberately curated) tool set rather than full API surface

evidence.source: Asana Forum Changelog - V2 MCP server GA
evidence.value: Active first-party investment: beta May 2025, post-incident rearchitecture, V2 GA Feb 2026, managed v1 sunset May 2026, native integrations in Claude and ChatGPT

strengths: Official Asana-hosted V2 remote (https://mcp.asana.com/v2/mcp) with Streamable HTTP and OAuth; GA since 2026-02-04
Post-incident rearchitecture: workspace-scoped authorizations, pre-registered OAuth apps (no DCR), and an optimized tool set replaced the flawed beta architecture
Broad client support including native Claude and ChatGPT integrations plus Cursor, VS Code, Claude Code, and Codex
Clear version lifecycle management: documented migration and explicit v1 shutdown date (2026-05-11)
Solid task/project operation reliability on the mature Asana REST API
limitations: SECURITY HISTORY: June 2025 cross-tenant incident - a flawed tenant-isolation check exposed tasks, project metadata, comments, and files of ~1,000 organizations to other tenants (Jun 5-17, 2025); the server was offline ~2 weeks and subsequently rearchitected as V2
Incident disclosure was private-notification only; no public statement - details reached the public via press and third-party researchers
Closed-source hosted multi-tenant service: the isolation logic that failed in 2025 remains externally unauditable
Admin app-management controls for MCP are limited to Enterprise+ / Legacy Enterprise; other tiers must contact support to block access
Task content, comments, attachments, and team member data flow to the LLM provider
V2 does not support OAuth Dynamic Client Registration - custom clients must pre-register in the developer console
MCP-specific audit visibility is limited outside Enterprise audit-log tiers

supported_platforms: Any MCP client with Streamable HTTP and OAuth support
metadata.authentication: OAuth with pre-registered apps (Dynamic Client Registration not supported in V2)
metadata.status: Active - V2 GA; v1/beta SSE server shut down 2026-05-11

security_incidents.name: MCP cross-tenant data exposure
security_incidents.summary: Flawed tenant-isolation check exposed data of ~1,000 organizations to other tenants between 2025-06-05 and 2025-06-17; server taken offline and rearchitected

code-generation.notes: Useful for issue-driven development when engineering work is tracked in Asana

customer-support.notes: Workable for support task tracking; incident history warrants caution with customer data in tasks

content-creation.notes: Good for editorial calendars and content production tracking

data-analysis.notes: Good for project status, workload, and portfolio reporting

research-assistant.notes: Useful for research project organization and task management

legal-compliance.notes: The 2025 cross-tenant exposure history makes this a hard sell for privileged matter tracking

healthcare.notes: Prior multi-tenant isolation failure argues against PHI-adjacent project data

financial-analysis.notes: Sensitive deal/finance project data carries elevated risk given incident history

education.notes: Good for coursework planning and group project coordination

creative-writing.notes: Useful for tracking writing pipelines and editorial tasks

best_for: Teams managing day-to-day work in Asana who want conversational task and project operations
Enterprise+ orgs that can govern MCP access through Asana app management
Project status reporting and workload summarization via Claude or ChatGPT native integrations
